_readme: Gospels Data — Jesus's life and ministry, compiled for the 'Jesus's World' interactive map (sister app of Paul's World). All dates AD. Coordinates [longitude, latitude]. Chronology follows a common reconstruction: baptism ~AD 29, four Passovers (John 2:13; 5:1; 6:4; 11:55), crucifixion Friday 14 Nisan = 3 April AD 33. Passion Week and Resurrection are encoded at day granularity as fractional years (1 day ~= 0.00274 yr). SEMANTIC REMAP of engine keys: 'journeys' = ministry periods; 'books' = marquee events/discourses (the 4-state chip system); 'churchEvents' = per-site event tracks, each with a `category` (miracle|teaching|encounter|event); 'paulEvents' = per-period narrative beats. `parables` is a separate thematic array (grouped by `theme`, each with ref/gospels/lesson and optional `occasion` cross-link), kept off the map because parables lack firm locations. Field names (writingLocationId, recipientCityIds, attribution, dateDebated, churchId) are kept as-is so the file is drop-in for the existing engine; see the code checklist for the copy relabel.

cities.name: Nazareth
cities.description: Jesus's hometown, where he grew up; twice rejected here — 'no prophet is accepted in his own country'

cities.name: Bethlehem
cities.description: City of David and place of Jesus's birth; the shepherds and the Magi

cities.name: Bethany beyond Jordan
cities.description: The Jordan ford where John baptized; site of Jesus's baptism and of his later withdrawal beyond the Jordan

cities.name: Judean Wilderness
cities.description: The desert where the Spirit led Jesus to be tempted forty days by the devil

cities.name: Cana
cities.description: Site of the first sign — water turned to wine — and of the healing of the official's son at a distance

cities.name: Capernaum
cities.fullName: Capernaum on the Sea of Galilee
cities.description: Jesus's ministry headquarters — 'his own city'; synagogue teaching, many healings, and the calling of Matthew

cities.name: Jerusalem
cities.description: The holy city and temple; goal of the feast pilgrimages and stage of the Passion, death, and resurrection

cities.name: Sychar
cities.description: Samaritan town by Jacob's well where Jesus spoke with the woman about living water; many believed

cities.name: Nain
cities.description: Town at whose gate Jesus raised a widow's only son from the funeral bier

cities.name: Chorazin
cities.description: Galilean town on which Jesus pronounced a woe for refusing to repent despite his mighty works

cities.name: Bethsaida
cities.description: Home of Peter, Andrew, and Philip; near here Jesus fed the five thousand and healed a blind man in stages

cities.name: Magdala
cities.description: Fishing town on the west shore, home of Mary Magdalene, from whom seven demons were cast out

cities.name: Gergesa
cities.description: East-shore region of the Decapolis where Jesus cast a legion of demons into the swine

cities.name: Tyre
cities.description: Phoenician coastal city; Jesus withdrew to its region, where the Syrophoenician woman's faith won her daughter's healing

cities.name: Sidon
cities.description: Northernmost point of Jesus's travels; he passed through Sidon toward the Decapolis

cities.name: Caesarea Philippi
cities.description: At the springs of the Jordan, Peter confessed 'You are the Christ'; here Jesus first foretold the cross

cities.name: Mount Hermon
cities.description: High mountain near Caesarea Philippi, the likely site of the Transfiguration before Peter, James, and John

cities.name: Jericho
cities.description: Oasis city on the ascent to Jerusalem; blind Bartimaeus received sight and Zacchaeus welcomed Jesus

cities.name: Ephraim
cities.description: Town near the wilderness where Jesus withdrew with the disciples as the plot against him hardened

cities.name: Bethany
cities.description: Home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus, whom Jesus raised; his lodging during Passion Week and place of the Ascension

cities.name: Bethphage
cities.fullName: Bethphage on the Mount of Olives
cities.description: Village on the Mount of Olives from which Jesus sent for the colt and began the Triumphal Entry

cities.name: Mount of Olives
cities.description: Ridge east of the temple; place of the Olivet Discourse over the city and, at its foot, the garden of Gethsemane

cities.name: Gethsemane
cities.description: Olive garden across the Kidron where Jesus prayed in agony and was betrayed and arrested

cities.name: Emmaus
cities.description: Village on the road from Jerusalem where the risen Jesus was made known to two disciples in the breaking of bread

cities.name: Tiberias
cities.fullName: Tiberias on the Sea of Galilee
cities.description: City giving its name to the lake; by its shore the risen Jesus gave the miraculous catch and restored Peter

cities.name: Mountain in Galilee
cities.description: The mountain in Galilee where the risen Jesus met the eleven and gave the Great Commission

journeys.name: Preparation & Early Ministry
journeys.description: From the Jordan baptism and wilderness temptation to the first sign at Cana, the first Passover cleansing of the temple, and the Samaritan mission — ending with rejection at Nazareth and the move to Capernaum

waypoints.note: Jesus leaves Nazareth, about thirty years old, and comes to the Jordan
Baptized by John; the Spirit descends as a dove and the Father's voice is heard
Led by the Spirit into the desert; forty days of fasting and temptation by the devil
The first disciples follow — Andrew, John, Simon Peter, Philip, Nathanael
The wedding: water becomes wine, the first of his signs; his glory revealed
Down to Capernaum with his mother, brothers, and disciples for a few days
First Passover: he cleanses the temple; Nicodemus comes by night ('you must be born again')
Passing through Samaria: the woman at the well; many Samaritans believe
Back in Cana he heals the royal official's son at Capernaum, at a distance — the second sign
In the synagogue he reads Isaiah; rejected and driven to the brow of the hill
He leaves Nazareth and settles in Capernaum, making it the base of the Galilean ministry

journeys.name: Great Galilean Ministry
journeys.description: Capernaum as headquarters: the calling of the Twelve, the Sermon on the Mount, circuits of Galilee with healings and parables, the storm stilled and Legion cast out, and the feeding of the five thousand at the Passover of John 6

waypoints.note: Calls Peter, Andrew, James, and John by the sea; drives out an unclean spirit; heals many at evening
'Let us go to the next towns' — preaching through the villages of Galilee
On the mountain he appoints the Twelve and gives the Sermon on the Mount to the crowds
At the town gate he raises a widow's only son; 'a great prophet has arisen among us'
Mary Magdalene and the women who followed and supported him out of their means
Teaches the parables from a boat; sends out the Twelve two by two
Crossing the sea he stills the storm, then casts the legion of demons into the swine
Back across the lake: Jairus's daughter raised, the woman with the flow of blood healed
At Passover he feeds the five thousand near Bethsaida, then walks on the sea
The Bread of Life discourse in the synagogue; many disciples turn back

journeys.name: Withdrawals & Beyond Galilee
journeys.description: Withdrawals into Gentile regions — Tyre and Sidon, the Decapolis, and the far north at Caesarea Philippi, where Peter confesses the Christ and the Transfiguration reveals his glory before the turn toward Jerusalem

waypoints.note: Into the region of Tyre: the Syrophoenician woman's faith wins her daughter's deliverance
Northward through Sidon, then back toward the Sea of Galilee by way of the Decapolis
In the Decapolis he heals a deaf man and feeds the four thousand
A blind man healed in two stages — first men 'like trees walking,' then clearly
'Who do you say that I am?' Peter: 'You are the Christ.' He foretells the cross
Transfigured before Peter, James, and John; Moses and Elijah appear; 'This is my beloved Son'
Home to Capernaum: the temple tax from the fish's mouth; 'who is the greatest?' and forgiveness

journeys.name: Later Judean & Perean Ministry
journeys.description: The road to Jerusalem: the feasts of Tabernacles and Dedication, the man born blind, the Perean ministry and parables, the raising of Lazarus, the withdrawal to Ephraim, and Jericho on the final ascent

waypoints.note: Feast of Tabernacles: 'if anyone thirsts, let him come to me'; the woman caught in adultery
Feast of Dedication: heals the man born blind; 'I and the Father are one'
Withdraws beyond the Jordan where John first baptized; the Perean parables — lost sheep, prodigal son
'Lazarus, come out' — raised after four days in the tomb; the Sanhedrin resolves on his death
No longer walking openly, he withdraws with the disciples to Ephraim near the wilderness
Blind Bartimaeus receives sight; Zacchaeus climbs the sycamore; 'today salvation has come to this house'
Six days before the Passover, Mary anoints his feet at Bethany for his burial

journeys.name: Passion Week
journeys.description: The final week in Jerusalem at day granularity — Triumphal Entry, the temple cleansed, the Olivet Discourse, the Last Supper and Gethsemane, the trials, and the crucifixion on Friday

waypoints.note: Sunday — the Triumphal Entry: he rides the colt into Jerusalem to shouts of 'Hosanna'
Monday — cleanses the temple a second time; curses the barren fig tree
Tuesday — temple confrontations by day; the Olivet Discourse on the end of the age
Wednesday — lodging at Bethany; Judas agrees to betray him for thirty pieces of silver
Thursday — the Last Supper in the upper room; the new covenant in his blood; the washing of feet
Thursday night — agony in the garden; 'not my will but yours'; betrayed with a kiss and arrested
Friday — trials before Caiaphas and Pilate; crucified at Golgotha; buried before the Sabbath

journeys.name: Resurrection Appearances
journeys.description: From the empty tomb through forty days of appearances — Mary Magdalene, the Emmaus road, the upper room, the shore of Galilee, the mountain of the Great Commission, and the Ascension from Bethany. Rendered dashed, like a journey reconstructed from many witnesses.

waypoints.note: Sunday at dawn — the empty tomb; he appears first to Mary Magdalene: 'do not cling to me'
That same day he walks unrecognized with two disciples, then is known in the breaking of bread
In the upper room, 'Peace be with you'; a week later Thomas: 'My Lord and my God'
By the Sea of Galilee: the miraculous catch of fish; Peter restored — 'Feed my sheep'
On the appointed mountain the eleven worship; the Great Commission — 'make disciples of all nations'
Forty days after Passover he leads them to Bethany, blesses them, and is taken up

books.name: The Baptism of Jesus
books.dateNote: Opens the public ministry; the Spirit descends and the Father's voice affirms the Son.
books.theme: The Father's voice and the Spirit anoint the Son at the threshold of his ministry

books.name: The Wedding at Cana
books.dateNote: The first of the signs in John's Gospel, near the start of the ministry.
books.theme: The first sign: water into wine reveals his glory and the disciples believe

books.name: Cleansing of the Temple
books.dateNote: John places a temple cleansing at the first Passover (2:13-22); the Synoptics place one in Passion Week. Whether these are one event or two is disputed.
books.theme: Zeal for the Father's house; 'destroy this temple and in three days I will raise it up'

books.name: The Woman at the Well
books.dateNote: On the return through Samaria after the first Passover.
books.theme: Living water offered across the Jew–Samaritan divide; worship in spirit and truth

books.name: The Sermon on the Mount
books.dateNote: The great discourse of the Galilean ministry (Luke's parallel is the Sermon on the Plain).
books.theme: The Beatitudes and the ethics of the kingdom — salt, light, and the Father's will

books.name: Calming the Storm
books.dateNote: During a crossing of the Sea of Galilee toward the Decapolis.
books.theme: 'Who then is this, that even wind and sea obey him?' — authority over chaos

books.name: Feeding the Five Thousand
books.dateNote: At the Passover of John 6 — the only miracle before the resurrection told in all four Gospels.
books.theme: Five loaves and two fish feed a multitude; 'I am the bread of life'

books.name: Peter's Confession
books.dateNote: At Caesarea Philippi, the hinge of the ministry toward the cross.
books.theme: 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God'; the first passion prediction

books.name: The Transfiguration
books.dateNote: About a week after Peter's confession, on a high mountain.
books.theme: His face shines; Moses and Elijah attend; 'This is my beloved Son; listen to him'

books.name: The Raising of Lazarus
books.dateNote: At Bethany shortly before Passion Week; in John, the sign that seals the plot to kill him.
books.theme: 'I am the resurrection and the life'; a dead man called out of the tomb

books.name: The Triumphal Entry
books.dateNote: Palm Sunday — the opening of Passion Week, in all four Gospels.
books.theme: The humble king on a colt; 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord'

books.name: The Olivet Discourse
books.dateNote: Delivered on the Mount of Olives in the last week, overlooking the temple.
books.theme: The fall of the temple and the coming of the Son of Man; 'watch therefore'

books.name: The Last Supper
books.dateNote: Thursday evening of Passion Week, in the upper room.
books.theme: The new covenant in bread and cup; the new commandment to love one another

books.name: The Crucifixion
books.dateNote: Friday, 14 Nisan; crucified at Golgotha outside the city wall.
books.theme: 'It is finished' — the death of the Son and the torn veil

books.name: The Resurrection
books.dateNote: The third day; the empty tomb and the first appearances.
books.theme: 'He is not here, for he has risen' — death undone and the tomb empty

churchEvents.label: Official's son healed
churchEvents.sublabel: The second sign, at a distance

churchEvents.label: First Catch of Fish
churchEvents.sublabel: The first catch of fish · Luke 5

churchEvents.label: 'I am the light of the world'
Woman Bent 18 Years
churchEvents.sublabel: Bent double for eighteen years · Luke 13

churchEvents.label: The servant's ear healed
churchEvents.sublabel: His last miracle before the cross

paulEvents.label: Baptized in the Jordan
paulEvents.sublabel: The Spirit descends as a dove

paulEvents.label: Man born blind healed
paulEvents.sublabel: 'I am the light of the world'

paulEvents.label: The Emmaus road
paulEvents.sublabel: Known in the breaking of bread

provinces.description: Herodian tetrarchies and neighboring regions during Jesus's ministry (~AD 29-33), replacing Paul's Roman provinces.
provinces.recommendedSource: Herodian-era boundaries; adapt from klokantech Roman Empire GeoJSON or AWMC political data for the reign of Tiberius.
provinces.renderingNotes: Render as semi-transparent fills; tint regions Jesus visited (Galilee, Judea, Samaria, Perea, Iturea, Decapolis, Phoenicia) with the 'provinceVisited' gold. Replace public/provinces.geojson with a Herodian dataset whose feature properties.name maps via normalizeProvinceName.

mapConfig.note: The whole drama fits within ~200 km — from Sidon (33.56N) to Bethlehem (31.7N), Emmaus (34.99E) to Mount Hermon (35.86E). Tune scale in MapView's d3.geoMercator so all sites sit inside the viewport; the 10m coastline swap matters most at this zoom.

parables.name: The Sower
parables.lesson: The same word bears fruit or not depending on the soil of the heart.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.locationNote: Taught from a boat near Capernaum.

parables.name: Weeds Among Wheat
parables.lesson: Good and evil grow together until the final harvest.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.name: Mustard Seed
parables.lesson: The kingdom starts small and grows beyond all expectation.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.name: The Leaven
parables.lesson: Hidden, quiet, total — the kingdom works through the whole.

parables.name: Hidden Treasure
parables.lesson: Worth selling everything to possess.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.name: Pearl of Great Price
parables.lesson: One find so precious the merchant liquidates all he has.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.name: The Dragnet
parables.lesson: The final sorting of the righteous and the wicked.

occasion.note: Taught in parables from a boat by the Sea of Galilee (Matthew 13)

parables.name: Growing Seed
parables.lesson: The seed grows the farmer knows not how — God gives the increase.

parables.name: The Lost Sheep
parables.lesson: Heaven rejoices over one sinner who repents.

occasion.note: Taught in the Perean ministry to tax collectors and sinners

parables.locationNote: Luke's setting on the journey; Matt 18 sets a parallel in Galilee.

parables.name: The Lost Coin
parables.lesson: God searches diligently for what is lost.

occasion.note: Taught in the Perean ministry to tax collectors and sinners

parables.name: The Prodigal Son
parables.lesson: The Father runs to welcome the returning sinner.

occasion.note: Taught in the Perean ministry to tax collectors and sinners

parables.name: Two Debtors
parables.lesson: He who is forgiven much loves much.

parables.name: Friend at Midnight
parables.lesson: Keep asking; persistence in prayer is honored.

parables.name: Persistent Widow
parables.lesson: Pray always and do not lose heart.

parables.name: Pharisee & Tax Collector
parables.lesson: The humble, not the self-righteous, go home justified.

parables.name: The Rich Fool
parables.lesson: Life is not measured by the abundance of possessions.

parables.name: The Shrewd Manager
parables.lesson: Use worldly wealth wisely for eternal ends; serve God, not money.

parables.name: Rich Man & Lazarus
parables.lesson: Indifference to the poor has eternal consequences.

parables.name: The Talents
parables.lesson: Entrusted, invested, accountable — faithfulness with what you're given.

occasion.note: Part of the Olivet Discourse in Passion Week

parables.name: The Minas
parables.lesson: A nobleman departs and returns to settle accounts.

parables.name: The Ten Virgins
parables.lesson: Be ready; the bridegroom comes at an unknown hour.

occasion.note: Part of the Olivet Discourse in Passion Week

parables.name: Wise & Foolish Builders
parables.lesson: Two houses, one storm — hearing versus doing his words.
parables.locationNote: Close of the Sermon on the Mount.

parables.name: The Great Banquet
parables.lesson: The invited make excuses; the highways and hedges are filled.
parables.locationNote: Matt 22 sets a royal parallel in Jerusalem.

parables.name: Faithful & Wise Servant
parables.lesson: Found working or found drunk — the master returns unannounced.

parables.name: Wicked Tenants
parables.lesson: They kill the vineyard owner's son — judgment on the leaders.
parables.locationNote: Taught in the temple during Passion Week.

parables.name: Barren Fig Tree
parables.lesson: One more year to bear fruit — patience with a limit.

parables.name: Sheep & Goats
parables.lesson: The nations are judged by mercy shown to the least.

occasion.note: Part of the Olivet Discourse in Passion Week

parables.locationNote: Close of the Olivet Discourse; part parable, part judgment vision.

parables.name: Unforgiving Servant
parables.lesson: Forgive as you have been forgiven.

occasion.note: Answering Peter on how often to forgive

parables.name: The Good Samaritan
parables.lesson: Love your neighbor, even across enemy lines.

occasion.note: Answering a lawyer on the Jerusalem-Jericho road

parables.name: New Cloth & Wineskins
parables.lesson: The new covenant cannot be patched onto the old system.

parables.name: Tower Builder & King at War
parables.lesson: Count the cost before you follow — discipleship is total.

parables.name: Lamp Under a Basket
parables.lesson: Light is meant to be set out, not hidden.

parables.name: Laborers in the Vineyard
parables.lesson: Grace is generous, not earned by the hours worked.
parables.locationNote: Taught on the way up to Jerusalem.

parables.name: The Two Sons
parables.lesson: One says no and obeys; one says yes and doesn't. Deeds over words.
parables.locationNote: Taught in the temple during Passion Week.
